data.title: Circle’s EURC grows as trade war pushes euro higher — Analyst
data.description:
The market cap of Circle’s Euro Coin (EURC), a euro-pegged stablecoin, is growing quickly as the ongoing trade war pushes the US dollar price lower. “In recent weeks, interest in the euro has grown tremendously” and “this interest has not escaped the Circle EURC stablecoin,” Obchakevich Research founder Alex Obchakevich wrote in a recent X post. The euro has risen by 2. 2%, reaching its highest price since February 2022 at its current price of $1
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/circle-s-eurc-grows-as-trade-war-pushes-euro-higher-analyst?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: SEC Delays Decision on Grayscale's Ethereum ETF Staking Function
data.description:
SEC postpones decision on Grayscale's Ethereum ETF staking, weighing significant market implications.
source: https://coincu.com/332121-sec-grayscale-ethereum-etf-staking

data.title: SEC Delays Decision on Grayscale Ethereum Spot ETF Staking Feature
data.description: According to Odaily, the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has postponed its decision regarding the approval of Grayscale's Ethereum Spot ETF staking feature. This delay comes amid ongoing discussions and evaluations of the proposed financial product. The SEC's decision is closely watched by market participants, as it could have significant implications for the cryptocurrency industry and investment strategies.

data.title: Bloomberg analyst: Canada plans to launch Solana spot ETF this week
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, wrote on the X platform that Canada is preparing to launch Solana spot ETFs this week after regulators approved several issuers including Purpose, Evolve, CI and 3iQ. These ETFs may include staking functions.

data.title: Deutsche Bank: Now expects the Fed to cut interest rates by 25 basis points in December
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News Deutsche Bank said it now expects the Federal Reserve to cut interest rates by 25 basis points in December, having previously expected no rate cuts in 2025. The Fed is expected to cut interest rates by two more 25 basis points in the first quarter of 2026, bringing the terminal rate to 3.5%-3.75%. (Jinshi)

data.title: Deutsche Bank Predicts December Rate Cut by Federal Reserve
data.description: According to Odaily, Deutsche Bank has revised its forecast, now anticipating a 25 basis point interest rate cut by the Federal Reserve in December. Previously, the bank had expected no rate cuts in 2025.

data.title: Vitalik Buterin Praises Aave's Resilience Amid Market Instability
data.description: According to Foresight News, Vitalik Buterin commended Aave's founder and CEO, Stani Kulechov, for effectively minimizing losses from hacking incidents. Buterin noted that Aave's loss levels are significantly lower than the average in the DeFi sector, being 50 times less than the industry average and 250 times less than centralized exchanges. He highlighted that Aave's actual annualized yield stands at -0.01%, compared to an expected yield of -0.53% and -2.75% for centralized exchanges. Amid recent macroeconomic and political instability, Buterin believes Aave and the DeFi sector are competitive in managing tail risks in the fiat domain.

data.title: US SEC delays approval of Grayscale Ethereum spot ETF staking function
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to market news: The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has postponed the approval of the Grayscale Ethereum spot ETF staking function.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Flat as Ray Dalio Warns 'Something Worse Than a Recession' Looming
data.description:
Dalio said the president's economic policies could lead to "an international conflict in a way that is highly disruptive to the world economy. "
source: https://decrypt.co/314689/bitcoin-price-ray-dalio-worse-than-recession

data.title: Investment bank Jefferies: Bitcoin mining profitability fell 7.4% in March
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Investment bank Jefferies data shows that Bitcoin mining profitability fell 7.4% in March due to an 11.2% drop in the average price of Bitcoin and a 9.1% drop in transaction fees.
Jefferies also said that U.S.-listed Bitcoin mining companies mined a total of 3,534 Bitcoins in March, compared with 3,002 in February. (CoinDesk)
